data.name: Rishabh Vishwakarma
intro: I learn in rabbit holes. One interesting question becomes a tutorial, becomes a half-built side project, becomes three new things I'm now tracking in parallel. Most of what I actually know came from exactly this loop.

I'm a CS student who is not tied to a single stack. I enjoy understanding how things work and building across [backend](https://github.com/Ris-git), cloud, DevOps, Android, and AI. Currently SDE Intern @ [Cyborn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cyborn-ltd/), and working on a few things I genuinely care about.

featured.name: Cyborn
body: As a software engineering intern operating across Android, web, and backend systems, I work on building communication products end-to-end — investigating performance bottlenecks, shaping user experiences, and turning ideas into shipped features.

list: Architecting and building core product features across Android and web platforms for a real-time communication ecosystem.
Tracing performance bottlenecks across client, backend, networking, and state-management layers to improve responsiveness and reliability.
Designing and refining user-facing experiences, discovery systems, navigation flows, and platform interactions across mobile and web.
Working with real-time systems, distributed data flows, caching strategies, and synchronization mechanisms that power modern communication platforms.
Operating top-to-bottom, from product exploration and technical investigation to implementation, testing, and deployment.

previous.name: Broadridge Financial Solutions
body: Trained in enterprise DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, release automation, and cloud infrastructure workflows.

list: Gained hands-on exposure to Docker, Linux, Jira, and core OS & networking fundamentals used in production systems.
Applied SRE and observability practices such as monitoring, centralized logging, alerting, incident response, and root cause analysis (RCA).
Developed understanding of reliability, scalability, security awareness, and Agile execution in a fintech environment.

previous.name: AWS Student Community
body: Active member participating in DevOps-focused events, hackathons, and cloud-skills sessions.

list: Focus areas: AWS, career development, and cloud skills.
Engaged with the community through workshops, technical meetups, and hands-on hackathons.

data.name: Serenity
data.subtitle: privacy-first mental health platform for universities
body: Most institutional software treats privacy as a policy. Serenity treats it as **architecture**. Admins cannot read therapy notes or student journals — not because of a rule, but because of how the access control is built.

list: **Role-based permissions enforced at the database level** with PostgreSQL Row Level Security.
Full-stack platform with role-based access, intervention workflows, and counselor support systems.
Translated research findings (peer-reviewed publication, IJSREM 2026) into product decisions across UX, engagement design, privacy, and system architecture.
Designed to feel like support, not surveillance.

data.name: ForecastGPT
data.subtitle: AI-driven forecasting meets portfolio optimisation
body: A production-style forecasting system that predicts market trends with **Prophet**, optimises portfolios, and explains insights through a natural-language AI assistant.

list: Automated testing with CI/CD on CircleCI.
Deployed on the cloud with a persistent SQL data layer for reliable, reproducible financial intelligence.
Natural-language LLM assistant surfaces insights from the forecast outputs.
MLOps practices applied end-to-end.

data.name: CI/CD Pipeline
data.subtitle: 3-tier web app, fully automated
body: **Jenkins → Ansible → Docker → Kubernetes → AWS**. Built end-to-end to understand how the whole system actually connects — not just configure individual tools in isolation.

list: Automated provisioning with Terraform and Ansible.
Container orchestration with Kubernetes on AWS.
Full CI/CD cycle from commit to production deployment.
Hands-on with the operational realities: networking, secrets management, rollout strategies.

items.title: Digital Mental Health and Psychological Support Systems for Students in Higher Education: A Comprehensive Review
items.venue: International Journal of Scientific Research in Engineering and Management (IJSREM), vol. 10, no. 4, pp. 1-5, April 2026
items.authors: Sangam; Ashwani Yadav; Rishabh Vishwakarma; Rishav Kumar Singh; Zeenat Zahra
items.abstract: Higher education institutions globally are confronting a mental health crisis of unprecedented scale and severity. The transition to university life coincides with the peak age of onset for a majority of mental health disorders. Longitudinal data reveal that, in the last decade alone, the number of college students reporting clinically significant symptoms has doubled; recent surveys indicate that as many as 70% of students have experienced mental health challenges since commencing their studies. In response, Digital Mental Health Interventions (DMHIs) have emerged as a critical and scalable solution. This comprehensive review synthesises the current evidence base on DMHIs for students in higher education, examining their effectiveness in reducing clinical symptoms and enhancing psychological well-being, analysing the critical challenge of user engagement and adherence, exploring implementation strategies and ethical considerations, and investigating emerging technologies including conversational AI, virtual reality, and passive sensing. The review concludes with strategic recommendations for university administrators, developers, and researchers to maximise the real-world impact of these interventions while navigating the complex ethical landscape of digital mental health care.

data.heading: Let's build something together
data.subheading: open to summer '26 internships, collaborations, and good conversations. drop me a line.
